
### Name Resolution System (the "Name Resolution" mentioned in branch name)

The current work focuses on implementing **user name resolution** - replacing raw user IDs with human-readable usernames throughout the UI:

#### **New Hook: `useUserMap.ts`** (lines 1-20)
```typescript
export function useUserMap() {
	const { activeOrg } = useOrganization();
	const { data: fullOrg, isLoading } = useFullOrganization(activeOrg?.id);

	const userMap = useMemo(() => {
		const record: Record<string, string> = {};
		if (fullOrg?.members) {
			for (const m of fullOrg.members) {
				record[m.userId] = m.user.name;  // Maps user ID → display name
			}
		}
		return record;
	}, [fullOrg]);

	return { userMap, isLoading };
}
```

#### **formatUsername Helper** (`lib/format.ts`, lines 14-22)
```typescript
export function formatUsername(
	userId: string,
	userMap?: Record<string, string>,
): string {
	if (userMap?.[userId]) {
		return userMap[userId];
	}
	return userId;  // Falls back to raw ID if no mapping exists
}
```

   - Queries user mappings via `orpc.analytics.users.mappings`

   - Simplified username lookup from `userMap.get(userId)` to `userMap[userId]`

Build and type checks pass. Here's what was wrong and what I fixed:

**Root cause:** In your theme CSS, `--muted` and `--muted-foreground` are set to the same color (`#73726c` in light, `#8e9991` in dark). Any element using `bg-muted text-muted-foreground` renders text invisible against the background — hence the "redacted" look on the scope badge and repository badge.

**Changes to `TimelineItem.tsx`:**
- Replaced the raw `<div>` card with shadcn `Card`/`CardHeader`/`CardContent` components
- Replaced all hand-rolled badge `<span>`s with the shadcn `Badge` component
- **Type badge** — uses `outline` variant with color-coded borders (green for pattern, red for antipattern, blue for convention, etc.)
- **Scope badge** — uses `secondary` variant (`bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground`) which has proper contrast
- **Repository badge** — same `secondary` variant fix
- **Tag badges** — uses `outline` with indigo border/text instead of hardcoded `bg-indigo-100`
